title: Infosys McCamish Resolves Oregon Data Breach Matter Through Assurance of Voluntary Compliance
detailed_summary: Infosys disclosed that Infosys McCamish Systems (' IMS '), a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, entered into an Assurance of Voluntary Compliance with the State of Oregon Attorney's General Office on August 5, 2025 regarding concerns tied to a 2023 data breach. The authority expressed concerns about potential violations of Oregon consumer protection laws, including alleged insufficient notice under the Oregon Consumer Information Protection Act. The matter was resolved without any admission or finding of violation, and IMS will pay $225,000 dollars to the Oregon Attorney General's Office.

key_facts.fact: On August 5, 2025, Infosys McCamish Systems (' IMS '), a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, entered into an Assurance of Voluntary Compliance with the State of Oregon Attorney's General Office.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The Oregon Attorney's General Office expressed concerns that IMS may have violated the Oregon Consumer Information Protection Act and the Oregon Unlawful Trade Practices Act with respect to a 2023 data breach.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The Assurance of Voluntary Compliance resolves the matter without any further action and specifically acknowledges that this is not an admission or finding that IMS violated the listed Acts.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: IMS will pay a sum of $225,000 dollars to the Oregon Attorney General's Office, with the full amount due within thirty business days of IMS' execution of the Assurance of Voluntary Compliance and its receipt of payment instructions.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident
